observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that: 1. the Road Inventory Survey Field Committee (RIC) to timely conduct an annual physical inventory count of the road networks to allow sufficient time for reconciliation with the accounting records; 2. the RIC to ensure the preparation and timely submission of the required Survey Field Sheet to the RPCLRN to the Office of the Auditor for subsequent reconciliation with the local road network property cards; 3. the MGSO, MEO, and MACCO to evaluate the existing processes in the identification of roads that qualify as PPE; to reconcile inventory records of both; and perform their duties and responsibilities articulated under COA Circular 2015-008 to properly account for local road networks; and 4. the Municipal Accounting Office to make the necessary adjustments, if necessary, to ensure that the Road Networks and its corresponding Accumulated Depreciation accounts are accurate; and to submit the adjustments made to the Audit Team.

We recommended and Management agreed that the MGSO and the Inventory and Disposal Committee to: 1. pursue its plan to implement the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts as provided in COA Circular No. 2020-006; and 2. facilitate the immediate disposal of unserviceable and obsolete properties and furnish the CAO with the necessary supporting documents for its derecognition from the books.

Ascertain the correct amount of issuances and ending balance of the Medical, Dental and Laboratory Supplies Inventory account as at December 31, 2024. Subsequently, prepare a report to be submitted to the MACCO for adjustments; 2. Make use of the shared Google Drive with the MHO for easy access of the movements of the inventories and ultimately ascertain the correct periodic balance; 3. Conduct complete and periodic physical count of inventories and reconcile the results with the stock cards; and 4. Prepare and submit the complete RPCI as required and perform reconciliation with MACCO. • MACCO: 5. Prepare adjusting entries once MGSO submits the completed report of the Medical, Dental and Laboratory Supplies Inventory; 6. Closely coordinate with the MGSO for the movements of inventories; and 7. Reconcile the ledger cards to the stock cards of the MGSO. We further recommended and Management also agreed that the Human Resource Head assess the manpower complement of the MGSO if it warrants hiring or assigning personnel who will prepare and maintain the necessary records.

We recommended and Management agreed to: 1. direct the MGSO to conduct physical count of these inventory accounts to concerned offices to ensure whether these items still exist; 2. direct the MACCO to prepare adjusting entries once MGSO submits the completed report of the affected accounts; 3. file a request for authority to write off dormant accounts with the Office of the COA Audit Team Leader (ATL) and/or Supervising Auditor (SA). The request must be duly supported by the documents enumerated in Section 8 of COA Circular 2023-008 and should follow an extensive process of verification, analysis, and validation.     b. Determine the persons responsible for the unsegregated waste disposal
       in  the  MRF  near  the  public   market   and   impose   appropriate
       administrative action/s, if necessary;


    c. Reconstitute the SWM Board including the following members:


       • Representative from NGOs whose  principal  purpose  is  to  promote
         recycling and the protection of air and water quality;
       • Representative from the recycling industry; and
       • Representative from the manufacturing or packaging industry


       However, if some cannot be included after exhausting all efforts, the
       Management can submit their justifications in writing;


    d. Fast track the closure of open dumpsite and  direct  the  Engineering
       Office  to  schedule  the  equipment  for  solid   waste   management
       operations;


    e. Direct the person/s responsible  for  the  non-closure  of  the  open
       dumpsite to pay the corresponding fines to the EMB;


    f.    Procure  appropriate  protective  equipment  for  those   directly
       performing solid waste management operations; and


    g. Reorient the relevant personnel  on  the  wearing  of  proper  attire
       during solid waste management operations.

    We recommended Management to:

     a. Instruct the concerned officers to submit the supplier’s request for
        supply and delivery time extension and the Agency’s approved request
        for extension of supply and delivery  time  or  any  documents  that
        would warrant  the  non-imposition  of  liquidated  damages  to  the
        concerned supplier. Otherwise, direct the  OIC-Municipal  Accountant
        to demand payment of the liquidated damages from the supplier.


     b. Require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to ensure the  completeness  of
        the supporting documents before submission to the COA Audit Team.

Recommendations:

    We recommended the Municipal Mayor to  instruct  the  newly  designated
Municipal Accountant to:

     a. Send formal demand letters to ZOFEMCO requesting them to submit  the
        required documents; and


     b. Explain to MBM  ZOFEMCO  the  deficiencies  noted  during  pre-audit
        conducted by the LGU.

Recommendations:

    We recommended Management to:


    a. Direct claimants with assigned  vehicles  to  refrain  from  claiming
       transportation allowances;


    b. Require the other  claimants  to  submit  a  certification  that  the
       official/employee did not use government vehicle and is not  assigned
       any government vehicle; and

    c. Submit the ARE of the vehicles listed in Annex K.

Recommendation:

    We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the  municipal  officials
concerned to adhere to the prescribed accounting guidelines and  to  prepare
required reports on all donations (cash and in-kind) received  from  various
donors as stipulated in the COA Circular 2014-002 dated April  15,  2014  to
achieve transparency and  accountability  in  the  use  of  disaster  relief
aid/donations.

Recommendations:

    We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:

      a. Require the Human Resource Development  Officer  (HRMO)  to  ensure
         that the provisions prescribed for a minimum monthly net  take  pay
         of P5,000.00 under the GAA FY 2019 and onwards be adhered to.

      b. Require the Municipal Accountant to strictly enforce the provisions
         stipulated in the GAA pertaining to net take-home pay to promote an
         efficient and effective public service by ensuring that  government
         employees have enough resources to support their  daily  sustenance
         until the next payday.


    c.     Advise the concerned employees to pay and/or  transact  directly
         with the private    institutions, if they opted to  avail  private
         loans, so that their net  take  home  pay  will  comply  with  the
         provision of the GAA.

Recommendations:

    We recommended Management to:


    a. Formulate a three-year program  indicating  strategic  prioritization
       policies in the allocation of the SEF to schools;


    b. Request the reports from DepEd containing the results of teacher  and
       classroom deployment analysis which will  be  one  of  the  basis  in
       prioritizing activities for proper allocation of the SEF;


    c. Refrain from charging the salaries and wages of  LSB  teacher/teacher
       aides assigned at District Offices to the SEF  of  the  Municipality;
       and


    d. Reconstitute the Local School Board in accordance with RA 7160.

Recommendations:

    We recommended Management to:

     a. Reassess the timelines in the procurement process of  the  Municipal
        Government in accordance with Annex C of  the  Revised  Implementing
        Rules and Regulation of Republic Act No. 9184; and


     b. Direct the office concerned to ensure  that  the  project  be  fully
        implemented as scheduled/programmed in the approved project proposal
        to attain the project’s goals and objectives on time.

Recommendations:

    We recommended Management to:

     a. Ensure that MLGU counterpart funds are allocated and transferred  to
        Trust Fund for the specific projects funded by  National  Government
        Agencies (NGAs);


     b. Proper coordination among concerned offices in the  preparation   of
        budgetary  requirements,  project  proposals  and  even  the  timely
        revision and approval thereof;


     c. Develop and adopt a monitoring tool for certain projects  funded  by
        the NGAs to determine the status of implementation; and

     d. Direct  the  office  concerned  to  ensure  that  project  be  fully
        implemented as scheduled/programmed in the approved project proposal
        to attain the project’s goals and objectives on time.

Recommendations:

    We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:

     a. Require the offices/officials concerned to identify the gaps in  the
        determination and implementation of development projects and thereby
        come up with corrective measures to ensure  optimum  utilization  of
        funds and timely delivery of  value-added services and benefits  to
        constituents;

     b.  Strengthen  the  communication  and  coordination   among   offices
        specifically during the planning phase to preclude confusion  during
        the implementation and monitoring of projects; and

     c.  Revisit  the  procurement  process  of  the  Municipality  that  is
        identified as one of the causes for delayed implementation.

the preparation of pertinent  reports  and  documents  are  coursed  through
proper offices, appropriate documents be used as basis for  tax  assessment,
set up and collections, in order to come up with a proper basis for the Tax
dues, RPT and SET Receivables during the year.

Also, we recommended to the Municipal Accountant to  record  receivables  on
RPT and SET on the basis of Certified List of Taxpayers and their  tax  dues
and to avoid the use of estimates  that  may  render  negative  or  abnormal
balances on the receivables. Likewise, we recommend the strict adherence  to
the above-mentioned guidelines.

Recommendations:

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the  Municipal  Engineer  to
reevaluate the statuses of completion of the above projects and prepare  the
necessary  Certificate  of  Completion  on  each  project  to  support   the
reclassification to the proper asset account.

Also, when found sufficient, we recommended to the Municipal  Accountant  to
prepare the journal entry for the transfer once  the  certificate  from  the
Municipal Engineering Office is issued.

And lastly, we recommended that proper coordination be made between  offices
so that deficiencies such as the subject-matter will be primarily  addressed
with.

Recommendations:

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor  to  direct  the  appropriate  offices
(MPDO and MAO) to:
1. Fast track the completion  of  the  above-mentioned  projects.  Regularly
   monitor the status of implementation of projects of this nature; and


2. Before entering into another  Memorandum  of  Agreement,  the  management
   should see to it that it has the capacity to implement the project within
   the time agreed and this can be done by evaluating first all the  aspects
   of the projects to avoid delays in its implementation.

Recommendations:

prepare the necessary adjusting entries for  the  prior  year’s  deficiency.

accounting and reporting guidelines of the MDRRMF.

and activities of the Municipality beneficial for its constituents.

120 dated July 4, 2012 for  the  strengthening  and  implementation  of  the
programs, projects and activities of the LCPC.

mentioned  criteria  and strictly adhere to their mandated provisions.

Environment and Natural Resources to provide, enforce, monitor and  evaluate
to enforce compliance on the wearing of the  complete,  basic  safety  gears

wastes inside the Municipal Dump Site.

will be appropriated and disbursed only for the  purpose/s  it  is  intended

liabilities and equity.

of Financial Position, Statement of Financial Performance and  Statement  of
Cash Flows) should be prepared and submitted regularly to  COA  as  provided
by above-stated guidelines.

Recommendations:

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct  the  Municipal  Development
Council to ensure an effective and proper planning on the utilization  of
the 20% LDF be made for the succeeding years.

We also recommended to the appropriate offices to  prepare  an  analysis  on
the adequacy and practicality of manpower complement,  construction  and
heavy  equipment  usage  of  the  Municipality  as  against  the  number  of
programs/projects administered by administration.

utilization of the 20% Local Development  Fund  for  the  implementation  of
various projects, programs and activities that are  envisioned  to  help  in
the attainment of desirable  socio-economic  development  and  environmental
outcomes and in accordance to the DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-1.
